org.arakhne.afc.ui.android.zoom

Class ZoomableView

  • java.lang.Object
    • android.view.View
      • org.arakhne.afc.ui.android.zoom.ZoomableView
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    android.graphics.drawable.Drawable.Callback, android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityEventSource, android.view.KeyEvent.Callback, ZoomableContext
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    FigureView

    public abstract class ZoomableView
extends android.view.View
implements ZoomableContext
    This abstract view provides the tools to move and scale the view. It is abstract because it does not draw anything.

    The implementation of the ZoomableView handles the pointer and key events as following:

    EventStatusCallbackNote
    POINTER_PRESSEDsupportedonPointerPressed(PointerEvent)Allways called
    POINTER_DRAGGEDsupportedonPointerDragged(PointerEvent)Called only when the scale and move gestures are not in progress
    POINTER_RELEASEDsupportedonPointerReleased(PointerEvent)Called only when the scale and move gestures are not in progress
    POINTER_MOVEDnot supportedPointer move on a touch screen cannot be detected?
    POINTER_CLICKnot supportedSee View.setOnClickListener(OnClickListener)
    POINTER_LONG_CLICKnot supportedSee View.setOnLongClickListener(OnLongClickListener)
    KEY_PRESSEDnot supportedSee View.onKeyDown(int, android.view.KeyEvent)
    KEY_RELEASEDnot supportedSee View.onKeyUp(int, android.view.KeyEvent)
    KEY_TYPEDnot supportedSee View.onKeyUp(int, android.view.KeyEvent)

    The function onDrawView(Canvas, float, CenteringTransform)} may use an instance of the graphical context DroidZoomableGraphics2D to draw the elements according to the zooming attributes.

    Version:
    $Name$ $Revision$ $Date$
    Author:
    $Author: galland$
    See Also:
    DroidZoomableGraphics2D
    Maven Group Id:
    $GroupId$
    Maven Artifact Id:
    $ArtifactId$

      Invoked when the a touch-up event is detected.

    • Method Detail

      • createDocumentWrapper

        protected abstract DocumentWrapper createDocumentWrapper()
        Invoked by the constructor to create a wrapper to the data of the viewed document. If there is no wrapper, several functions of ZoomableView will not work.
        Returns:
        the wrapper or null.

      • isXAxisInverted

        public final boolean isXAxisInverted()
        Specified by:
        isXAxisInverted in interface ZoomableContext

      • isYAxisInverted

        public final boolean isYAxisInverted()
        Specified by:
        isYAxisInverted in interface ZoomableContext

      • setXAxisInverted

        public final void setXAxisInverted(boolean invert)
        Invert or not the X axis.

        If the X axis is inverted, the positives are to the left; otherwise they are to the right (default UI standard).

        Parameters:
        invert -

      • setYAxisInverted

        public final void setYAxisInverted(boolean invert)
        Invert or not the Y axis.

        If the Y axis is inverted, the positives are to the left; otherwise they are to the right (default UI standard).

        Parameters:
        invert -

      • isIgnoreRepaint

        public final boolean isIgnoreRepaint()
        Replies if the repaint requests are ignored or not.
        Returns:
        true if the repaint requests are ignored; false if not.

      • repaint

        public final void repaint()
        Invalidate this view wherever this function is invoked. If the current thread is the UI-thread, View.invalidate() is invoked. If not, View.postInvalidate() is invoked.

        If isIgnoreRepaint() replies false, this function does nothing.

        See Also:
        isIgnoreRepaint()

      • repaint

        public final void repaint(float x,
           float y,
           float width,
           float height)
        Invalidate this view wherever this function is invoked. If the current thread is the UI-thread, View.invalidate() is invoked. If not, View.postInvalidate() is invoked.

        If isIgnoreRepaint() replies false, this function does nothing.

        Parameters:
        x -
        y -
        width -
        height -
        See Also:
        isIgnoreRepaint()

      • logical2pixel

        public Shape2f logical2pixel(Shape2f s)
        Specified by:
        logical2pixel in interface ZoomableContext

      • pixel2logical

        public Shape2f pixel2logical(Shape2f s)
        Specified by:
        pixel2logical in interface ZoomableContext

      • getScalingSensitivity

        public final float getScalingSensitivity()
        Specified by:
        getScalingSensitivity in interface ZoomableContext

      • setScalingSensitivity

        public final void setScalingSensitivity(float sensivility)
        Replies the sensivility of the zoomIn() and zoomOut() actions.
        Parameters:
        sensivility -

      • getViewportCenterX

        public final float getViewportCenterX()
        Replies the X coordinate of the center of the viewport (in screen coordinate).
        Returns:
        the center of the viewport.

      • getViewportCenterY

        public final float getViewportCenterY()
        Replies the Y coordinate of the center of the viewport (in screen coordinate).
        Returns:
        the center of the viewport.

      • getFocusX

        public final float getFocusX()
        Specified by:
        getFocusX in interface ZoomableContext

      • getFocusY

        public final float getFocusY()
        Specified by:
        getFocusY in interface ZoomableContext

      • getScalingFactor

        public final float getScalingFactor()
        Specified by:
        getScalingFactor in interface ZoomableContext

      • setScalingFactor

        public final boolean setScalingFactor(float factor)
        Set the scaling factor.
        Parameters:
        factor - is the scaling factor.
        Returns:
        true if the scaling factor has changed; otherwise false.

      • setScalingFactorForPixelRatio

        public void setScalingFactorForPixelRatio(float ratio)
        Change the scaling factor to have the specified ratio between 1 pixel and 1 unit in the document.

        Each unit from the displayed document will have the same graphical size as the amount of pixels specified by the ratio.

        Parameters:
        ratio -

      • setScalingFactorAndFocus

        protected final boolean setScalingFactorAndFocus(float scalingX,
                               float scalingY,
                               float factor)
        Set the scaling factor. This function does not repaint.
        Parameters:
        scalingX - is the coordinate of the point (on the screen) where the focus occurs.
        scalingY - is the coordinate of the point (on the screen) where the focus occurs.
        factor - is the scaling factor.
        Returns:
        true if the scaling factor or the focus point has changed; otherwise false.

      • zoomIn

        public final void zoomIn()
        Zoom the view in.

      • zoomOut

        public final void zoomOut()
        Zoom the view out.

      • getMaxScalingFactor

        public final float getMaxScalingFactor()
        Specified by:
        getMaxScalingFactor in interface ZoomableContext

      • setMaxScalingFactor

        public final void setMaxScalingFactor(float factor)
        Set the maximal scaling factor allowing in the view
        Parameters:
        factor - is the maximal scaling factor.

      • getMinScalingFactor

        public final float getMinScalingFactor()
        Specified by:
        getMinScalingFactor in interface ZoomableContext

      • setMinScalingFactor

        public final void setMinScalingFactor(float factor)
        Set the minimal scaling factor allowing in the view
        Parameters:
        factor - is the minimal scaling factor.

      • setFocusPoint

        public final void setFocusPoint(float x,
                 float y)
        Set the position of the focus point.
        Parameters:
        x -
        y -

      • translateFocusPoint

        public final void translateFocusPoint(float dx,
                       float dy)
        Translate the position of the focus point.
        Parameters:
        dx -
        dy -

      • onUpdateViewParameters

        protected void onUpdateViewParameters()
        Update any viewing parameter according to the current value of the focus point and the scaling factor.

        This function is invoked when the coordinates of the focus point or the scaling factor has been changed to ensure that all the drawing attributes are properly set.

      • getPreferredFocusX

        protected abstract float getPreferredFocusX()
        Replies the preferred position of the focus point.
        Returns:
        the preferred position of the focus point.

      • getPreferredFocusY

        protected abstract float getPreferredFocusY()
        Replies the preferred position of the focus point.
        Returns:
        the preferred position of the focus point.

      • resetView

        public final boolean resetView()
        Reset the view to the default configuration.
        Returns:
        true if the view has changed; false otherwise.

      • getScalingFactorToFit

        public float getScalingFactorToFit()
        Replies the scaling factor that may be used to fit the content of the document to the drawing area.

        If there is no wrapper replied by createDocumentWrapper(), this function replies 1.

        Returns:
        the scaling factor that permits to fit the document.

      • fitView

        public final boolean fitView()
        Reset the view so that the document is fitting the drawing area.

        If there is no wrapper replied by createDocumentWrapper(), this function does the same as resetView().

        Returns:
        true if the view has changed; false otherwise.

      • onSizeChanged

        protected void onSizeChanged(int w,
                 int h,
                 int oldw,
                 int oldh)
        Overrides:
        onSizeChanged in class android.view.View

      • onDraw

        public final void onDraw(android.graphics.Canvas canvas)
        Overrides:
        onDraw in class android.view.View

      • onDrawView

        protected abstract void onDrawView(android.graphics.Canvas canvas,
              float scaleFactor,
              CenteringTransform centeringTransform)
        Invoked to paint the view after it is translated and scaled.
        Parameters:
        canvas - is the canvas in which the view must be painted.
        scaleFactor - is the scaling factor to use for drawing.
        centeringTransform - is the transform to use to put the draws at the center of the view.

      • onTouchEvent

        public final boolean onTouchEvent(android.view.MotionEvent ev)
        Overrides:
        onTouchEvent in class android.view.View

      • onPointerPressed

        protected void onPointerPressed(PointerEvent e)
        Invoked when the a touch-down event is detected.
        Parameters:
        e -

      • onPointerReleased

        protected void onPointerReleased(PointerEvent e)
        Invoked when the a touch-up event is detected.
        Parameters:
        e -

      • onPointerDragged

        protected void onPointerDragged(PointerEvent e)
        Invoked when the pointer is moved with a button down.
        Parameters:
        e -

      • onLongClick

        protected void onLongClick(PointerEvent e)
        Invoked when a long-click is detected.
        Parameters:
        e -

      • onClick

        protected void onClick(PointerEvent e)
        Invoked when a short-click is detected.
        Parameters:
        e -

      • onScale

        public boolean onScale(float focusX,
              float focusY,
              float requestedScaleFactor)
        Invoked when the view must be scaled.

        One of the border effect if this function replies true is that the view will be repaint.

        The default implementation of this function invokes setScalingFactorAndFocus(float, float, float).

        Parameters:
        focusX - is the position of the focal point on the screen.
        focusY - is the position of the focal point on the screen.
        requestedScaleFactor - is the new scale factor.
        Returns:
        Whether or not the detector should consider this event as handled. If an event was not handled, the detector will continue to accumulate movement until an event is handled. This can be useful if an application, for example, only wants to update scaling factors if the change is greater than 0.01.
        See Also:
        setScalingFactorAndFocus(float, float, float)

      • toast

        public final void toast(String message,
         boolean isLong)
        Show a toast message.
        Parameters:
        message - is the message to display
        isLong - indicates if it is a long-time (true) or a short-time (false) message.

      • toast

        public final void toast(int message,
         boolean isLong)
        Show a toast message.
        Parameters:
        message - is the message to display
        isLong - indicates if it is a long-time (true) or a short-time (false) message.
